tool.name: RGI bwt
tool.description: align metagenomic reads to CARD to identify AMR genes
tool.command: mkdir -p staging && rgi load @CMD_CARD_LOAD_ARGS@ @CMD_WILDCARD_LOAD_ARGS@ --local && rgi bwt -1 '$read_one' #if $read_two -2 '$read_two' #end if -a '$aligner' -n \${GALAXY_SLOTS:-4} -o staging/output $include_wildcard $include_other_models #if str($read_filters.mapq).strip() --mapq $read_filters.mapq #end if #if str($read_filters.mapped).strip() --mapped $read_filters.mapped #end if #if str($read_filters.coverage).strip() --coverage $read_filters.coverage #end if --local --clean

param.name: read_one
param.label: Read 1 (or single-end reads)
param.help: Quality-trimmed FASTQ reads. For single-end sequencing, provide only this file.

param.name: read_two
param.label: Read 2 (paired-end, optional)
param.help: Reverse reads for paired-end sequencing. Leave empty for single-end.

param.name: aligner
param.label: Read aligner
param.help: kma is optimised for metagenomics and handles redundant reference sequences efficiently. bowtie2 and bwa produce standard SAM/BAM output and are suitable when downstream tools require those formats.

param.label: Include wildcard variants (WildCARD)
param.help: Also align reads against in silico predicted AMR gene variants from WildCARD. Improves detection of resistance variants not yet in canonical CARD. Requires a WildCARD database to be loaded above.

param.label: Include other model types
param.help: Also align against protein variant, rRNA variant, knockout, and protein overexpression models in addition to the default homolog models. Requires the 'All model types' annotation FASTA to be loaded for CARD (and WildCARD if enabled).

param.label: Minimum MAPQ score
param.help: Discard alignments below this mapping quality score. MAPQ 0 indicates a non-unique alignment; MAPQ 20 corresponds to a 1% chance of mis-mapping. Leave empty to keep all alignments.

param.label: Minimum mapped read count
param.help: Discard AMR references with fewer than this many mapped reads. Useful for removing low-confidence hits in low-depth samples. Leave empty to disable.

param.label: Minimum reference coverage (%)
param.help: Discard AMR references where fewer than this percentage of the reference length is covered by reads. Typical cutoffs range from 80 to 100%. Leave empty to disable.

tool.help:
**RGI bwt — Metagenomic Read Alignment to CARD**
Aligns quality-trimmed short reads directly to the Comprehensive Antibiotic Resistance Database (CARD) to identify and quantify AMR genes in metagenomic samples. Optionally extends alignment to predicted allelic variants (WildCARD).
-----
**Inputs**
- **Read 1** — quality-trimmed FASTQ reads (R1 for paired-end, or all reads for single-end). gzip-compressed FASTQ is accepted. - **Read 2** — reverse reads for paired-end sequencing (optional). Omit for single-end data. - **CARD database** — Select a pre-installed CARD release or supply files from your history. Read alignment requires a CARD annotation FASTA in addition to ``card.json``. Select *Homolog models only* for standard analysis, or *All model types* if you also enable **Include other model types**. - **WildCARD database** — Optional. Provide to enable alignment against predicted AMR gene variants (see WildCARD section below).
-----
**Read aligner**
- *kma* (default, recommended) — k-mer-based aligner optimised for metagenomics. Efficiently handles redundant reference sequences and is the fastest option. - *bowtie2* — short-read aligner suitable for Illumina data. Produces standard SAM output. - *bwa* — general-purpose short-read aligner. Use when downstream tools require bwa-compatible BAM format.
-----
**WildCARD variants**
Enable *Include wildcard variants* to also align reads against in silico predicted allelic variants from the WildCARD database. WildCARD captures resistance gene diversity not yet represented in canonical CARD, improving detection of novel or divergent variants. Requires a WildCARD database to be loaded above.
-----
**Include other model types**
By default only homolog models are searched. Enable *Include other model types* to also align against protein variant, rRNA variant, knockout, and protein overexpression models. Requires the *All model types* annotation FASTA to be loaded for both CARD and (if enabled) WildCARD.
-----
**Read mapping filters**
Post-alignment filters applied before results are reported. Leave any filter empty to disable it.
- *Minimum MAPQ* — discard alignments below this mapping quality score. MAPQ 0 indicates a non-unique alignment; MAPQ 20 corresponds to a ~1% chance of mis-mapping. A value of 1 removes completely ambiguous alignments. - *Minimum mapped read count* — discard AMR references with fewer than this many mapped reads. Useful for removing low-confidence hits in low-depth samples. - *Minimum reference coverage* — discard AMR references where fewer than this percentage of the reference length is covered by reads. Typical cutoffs range from 80 to 100%.
-----
**Outputs**
- **Allele mapping (tabular/JSON)** — per-allele statistics: read counts, RPM, percent identity, percent coverage, and AMR gene family. The JSON format contains the same data and is suitable for downstream parsing. - **Gene mapping (tabular)** — read counts and coverage aggregated to the gene level across all alleles. - **Overall mapping stats** — summary BAM statistics for the run (total reads, mapped reads, mapping rate). - **Reference mapping stats** — per-reference-sequence alignment statistics from the aligner. - **Artifacts mapping stats** — reads aligning to artifact sequences (e.g. plasmid backbones used as internal controls). Non-zero counts may indicate contamination or mis-classified sequences.
-----
**References**
Alcock et al. (2020). CARD 2020: antibiotic resistome surveillance with the Comprehensive Antibiotic Resistance Database. *Nucleic Acids Research*, 48(D1), D517–D525. https://doi.org/10.1093/nar/gkz935
RGI GitHub: https://github.com/arpcard/rgi
